_incidents.title: Results-dir race: two concurrent same-corruption-mode jobs silently mixed checkpoints
_incidents.description: Launched 1:2 and 1:3 gaussian:mask sanity runs (both corruption_mode=mixture) within the same second. corruption_sanity.sbatch keys RESULTS_DIR only by CORRUPTION_MODE (not job ID), and train.py's experiment_index = len(glob(RESULTS_DIR/*)) is a classic TOCTOU race -- both processes computed index=002 and wrote to the SAME .../corruption_sanity_mixture/002-EqM-B-2-Linear-velocity-None/ directory, confirmed via both jobs' logs showing identical 'Experiment directory created at ...002-...' at the same timestamp (2026-07-07 11:31:44). Both jobs ran to completion (exit 0, 8 checkpoints present at steps 5000-40000) but each checkpoint file's actual weights (1:2 vs 1:3 mask ratio) are INDETERMINATE -- whichever process's torch.save() won the race for that step. These checkpoints must NOT be used for eval; discarding entirely.
_incidents.root_cause: corruption_sanity.sbatch RESULTS_DIR keyed by CORRUPTION_MODE only; train.py experiment_index has no locking/atomicity for concurrent writers to the same results_dir.
_incidents.fix: corruption_sanity.sbatch RESULTS_DIR now includes ${SLURM_JOB_ID} unconditionally, eliminating any possibility of two jobs sharing a results dir regardless of corruption_mode or submission timing. Committed 17217f4.
_incidents.recovery_action: Relaunched both runs cleanly as jobs 29233398 (1:2) and 29233403 (1:3) with the fixed sbatch.
_incidents.lesson: Never launch two jobs with the same corruption_mode close together without a job-scoped results dir -- this applies retroactively to the earlier arm-B mixture run (job 28888839) and the original 3-arm mixture run (job 28814819), which were NOT launched concurrently with same-mode siblings so are believed unaffected, but the results-dir-collision risk existed for all of them before this fix.

completed_runs.description: cross-corruption generalization eval on gaussian+mask 1:4 ckpt -- final checkpoint in the generalization suite (6 of 6 done: gaussian, mask, 1:1, 1:2, 1:3, 1:4).
completed_runs.gate: cross_corruption_generalization
completed_runs.final_metric: recovery_mse: mask_p0.25=0.096, mask_p0.75=0.213, mask_p0.9=0.307, block_mask=0.659, stroke_mask=0.649, sigma0.3=0.053, sigma0.6=0.121, sigma1.0=0.203, noisy_masked=0.166. Non-monotonic pattern confirmed: 1:4's block_mask=0.659 DOES clear the compositional bar (beats mask-only 0.666 and gaussian 0.768), just not as strongly as arm B's 0.649 -- so 1:1 and 1:4 both clear it, 1:2/1:3 dip below in between. noisy_masked=0.166 beats gaussian (0.229) and arm B (0.169) but not mask-only (0.160) -- no mixture arm beats mask-only on noisy_masked yet. ordering_holds=TRUE for 8/9 corruptions (fails only mask_p0.9) -- tied with arm B for best ordering-stability profile (corrected from an earlier miscounted claim of 9/9 perfect).

Does NOT clear the compositional bar arm B cleared: block_mask=0.681 is worse than BOTH mask-only (0.666) and arm B (0.649); noisy_masked=0.163 beats gaussian (0.229) but not mask-only (0.160) or arm B (0.169, close). ordering_holds fails on mask_p0.9 and sigma1.0 (2/9), same pattern as other mixture arms.

completed_runs.description: cross-corruption generalization eval on gaussian+mask 1:3 ckpt.
completed_runs.gate: cross_corruption_generalization
completed_runs.final_metric: recovery_mse: mask_p0.25=0.103, mask_p0.75=0.224, mask_p0.9=0.317, block_mask=0.668, stroke_mask=0.625, sigma0.3=0.055, sigma0.6=0.121, sigma1.0=0.205, noisy_masked=0.171. Also does NOT clear the compositional bar: block_mask=0.668 barely worse than mask-only's 0.666 (essentially tied, does not beat it), well worse than arm B's 0.649. noisy_masked=0.171 worse than both mask-only (0.160) and arm B (0.169). PATTERN EMERGING: only arm B (1:1, equal weighting) shows the block_mask compositional-generalization win -- 1:2 and 1:3 trade it away for better in-distribution (trained-corruption) recovery gap, even though their raw Pareto numbers (gap/FID) look better than arm B's.

completed_runs.description: Phase 2 multi-seed replicate: arm B (1:1 gaussian:mask), seed=1. User requested replicates after seeing that the compositional-generalization effect sizes (e.g. block_mask 0.649 vs mask-only 0.666, a 2.5% relative gap) are small enough to plausibly be single-seed noise. Testing whether the block_mask 'beats both parents' result and the Pareto gap/FID numbers hold across seeds. Required fixing corruption_sanity.sbatch first -- GLOBAL_SEED was not plumbed through to train.py --global-seed at all (silent no-op), fixed same commit. Originally job 29403281 on seas_gpu (ETA ~18:16, ~4.5h queue wait) -- moved to gpu_test (idle, immediate start) for a guaranteed ~6h total instead of a queue-estimate gamble.
completed_runs.gate: phase2_multiseed_confirmation

next_action: Pareto sweep (1:1-1:4) AND cross-corruption generalization suite (6 checkpoints x 9 corruptions) both complete -- see gates.pareto_sweep_complete and gates.cross_corruption_generalization. Central finding: raw Pareto-optimality (1:3/1:4 win on trained-corruption gap+FID) and compositional generalization (only 1:1 and 1:4 beat both pure arms on block_mask) point to different recipes -- 1:2/1:3 are Pareto-better but generalize worse than 1:1, while 1:4 is good on both axes. Awaiting user decision (needs_user_input) on which framing the paper leads with, since that determines the recipe to carry into Phase 2 multi-seed confirmation. No further compute should be launched until that choice is made -- do not invent new sweep points or eval types without explicit user direction.

gates.step0_smoke: PASS - all 4 corruption modes run without crash, finite loss, on real IN-1K
gates.step2_mask_sanity: PASS (2026-07-06) - masked-recovery eval, mask_prob=0.5, n=256 held-out IN-1K val images, seed=0 (RNG-matched across arms). mean_masked_mse: gaussian floor=0.24237, mask treatment=0.11313, VAE-roundtrip oracle=0.01488. normalized_gap = (floor-treatment)/(floor-oracle) = 0.568. Mask-trained model recovers masked regions ~2.1x better than gaussian-trained model (57% of the floor-to-oracle headroom closed) -- masking the training start-state teaches the model to use visible context, which pure-Gaussian training never sees. Loss-curve comparison from earlier (mask ~5.2 vs gaussian ~11.2) is NOT part of this verdict -- retracted separately as an easiness artifact of the training target, unrelated to this outcome-metric-based PASS.
gates.step3_fourier_sanity: RESOLVED, NEGATIVE (2026-07-06) - isolated fourier-corruption training (fourier-cutoff=0.25), same eval protocol as step2. mean_masked_mse=0.40612, WORSE than gaussian floor (0.24237). normalized_gap=-0.720 (negative -- fourier-trained model is worse at masked-recovery than a model that never saw structured corruption at all). Mechanism read: fourier low-pass corruption teaches recovery of missing HIGH-FREQUENCY detail from coarse structure, not recovery of a spatially-masked region from surrounding visible pixels -- these are different tasks, and training on one does not transfer to (and may actively hurt, via distribution mismatch) the other. USER DECISION 2026-07-07: fourier dropped from build path entirely (option a of pi-updates.md ask).
gates.step4_mixture_arm_B: RESOLVED (2026-07-06) - 2-way gaussian+mask mixture (weights 1.0/1.0/0.0), same eval protocol. mean_masked_mse=0.13692, normalized_gap=0.464. Falls between isolated mask (0.568) and 3-arm mixture (0.449) as expected, but notably closer to the 3-arm mixture than to isolated mask -- confirms the 3-arm mixture's underperformance vs isolated mask is NOT primarily fourier-specific drag (arm B still loses ~0.10 gap vs pure mask despite excluding fourier entirely). Read together with step3's negative fourier result: the gap-closing recipe (gaussian:mask:fourier 1:1:1 vs 1:1:0 vs mask-only) suggests BOTH effects are real -- gradient-budget dilution (any mixing with gaussian reduces the pure-mask signal) AND fourier's own negative contribution. Fourier's negative pull is smaller in magnitude here (only 1/3 weight) than shown by its isolated -0.720 result, consistent with a real but diluted drag.
gates.generation_quality_fid: RESOLVED (2026-07-07) - sanity FID (2000 samples, unconditional, GD sampler 250 steps, single seed) on gaussian/mask/arm-B checkpoints. Smoke-sample-probe passed (eyeballed generated images: coherent textured scenes, not garbage/noise -- confirms sampler correctness at this early-training FID range). Results: gaussian floor FID=172.567, mask-only FID=239.512 (+66.9), gaussian+mask arm B FID=176.711 (+4.1). CONFIRMS user's hypothesis: mask-only trades substantial generation quality for its larger masked-recovery gain (0.568 vs arm B's 0.464, a 0.104 difference) -- gaussian+mask nearly preserves baseline generation quality while capturing 82% of mask-only's recovery gain. This is the key generalization-vs-specialization tradeoff the user asked to test; it is a genuine tradeoff, not a clean winner for either recipe.
gates.energy_ordering: RESOLVED (2026-07-07) - field-norm proxy (||f(x)||, since ebm=none has no scalar energy head) on gaussian/mask/arm-B checkpoints, n=256, mask_prob=0.5. Checks E(clean) < E(corrupt) < E(noise) per CLAUDE.md diagnostic 3. clean < corrupt holds for all three arms (all recognize near-manifold states as low-field). But corrupt vs noise ordering ONLY holds for arm B (gaussian+mask): gaussian floor has corrupt=304.2 > noise=250.1 (FAILS, never trained on masked states -- off-distribution), mask-only has corrupt=202.6 > noise=182.3 (FAILS, never trained on pure noise -- off-distribution), arm B has corrupt=199.9 < noise=248.4 (HOLDS). This is a SECOND, mechanistically independent line of evidence for the same generalization argument as generation_quality_fid: training on both corruption types produces a model whose field response is well-calibrated across the whole state space, not just one whose FID happens to be preserved. Single-arm checkpoints each show a specific off-distribution failure mode matching exactly what they were never trained on.
gates.pareto_sweep_complete: RESOLVED (2026-07-08) - gaussian:mask ratio sweep 1:1/1:2/1:3/1:4, all sanity scale (1 epoch/40k steps, EqM-B/2, single seed), same masked-recovery + FID protocol as generation_quality_fid. Full table: gaussian floor gap=0.0/FID=172.567; arm B (1:1) gap=0.464/FID=176.711; 1:2 gap=0.502/FID=176.190; 1:3 gap=0.513/FID=177.996; 1:4 gap=0.518/FID=178.511; mask-only gap=0.568/FID=239.512. FINDING: 1:2, 1:3, and 1:4 all Pareto-DOMINATE arm B -- strictly higher recovery gap AND equal-or-lower FID, not a tradeoff. Gap keeps climbing monotonically 1:1->1:4 (0.464->0.518) while FID stays flat/near-floor (176.2-178.5, all within +6 of gaussian floor, nowhere near mask-only's +66.9). The trend has NOT reversed by 1:4 -- ceiling not yet found; user did not pre-approve testing 1:5+, so sweep stops here by design, not because performance degraded. One incident during this sweep: a results-dir race corrupted the first 1:2/1:3 attempt (see _incidents), caught and fixed (RESULTS_DIR now keyed by SLURM_JOB_ID), all reported numbers are from the clean relaunch.
gates.cross_corruption_generalization: RESOLVED (2026-07-08) - built eval_generalization.py (new, per user request) testing whether gaussian+mask learns a genuinely general repair field vs memorizing its two exact training corruptions. All 6 checkpoints (gaussian, mask, 1:1, 1:2, 1:3, 1:4) evaluated on 9 unseen/composed corruptions (Bernoulli mask p=0.25/0.75/0.9 vs trained p=0.5, block mask, stroke mask, gaussian noise sigma=0.3/0.6/1.0, and noisy_masked -- a composition matching neither pure training distribution). KEY RESULT: on block_mask (recovery_mse), gaussian=0.768, mask-only=0.666, arm B(1:1)=0.649, 1:2=0.681, 1:3=0.668, 1:4=0.659. Only 1:1 and 1:4 beat BOTH pure arms simultaneously (genuine compositional generalization, not just interpolating between two memorized skills) -- 1:2 and 1:3 fall back above mask-only's floor despite having BETTER raw Pareto numbers (higher gap, lower FID) than 1:1. This is a real, non-monotonic tension: Pareto-optimality on trained corruptions and compositional generalization to novel corruptions are NOT the same axis and do not co-optimize cleanly across this ratio sweep. On noisy_masked, no mixture arm yet beats mask-only (0.160) -- gaussian=0.229, arm B=0.169, 1:2=0.163, 1:3=0.171, 1:4=0.166, all mixtures beat gaussian but not mask-only. Field-ordering (ordering_holds) is best for arm B AND 1:4 (both 8/9, fail only mask_p0.9), better than gaussian (4/9), mask-only (5/9), and 1:2/1:3 (7/9 each, fail mask_p0.9 and gaussian_sigma1.0). Corrected 2026-07-08: an earlier draft of this entry miscounted arm B as 6/9 and 1:4 as a 'perfect 9/9' -- both wrong, verified by direct recount from raw JSON; both are actually tied at 8/9. Smoke-sample-probe not separately re-run for this eval (reuses the same GD sampler/checkpoints already validated by generation_quality_fid).

phase2_blur_extension_seed0_gate.note: FID (3-seed mean): gaussian 173.49+-1.38, blur-only 387.80+-1.61, 1:1-blur 188.72+-2.04 (+15.2 over gaussian, fails +10 threshold), 1:4-blur 208.07+-2.00 (+34.6, fails). Blur-recovery MSE @ sigma=1.1029 (3-seed mean): blur-only 0.03403+-0.00079, 1:1 0.03904+-0.00038 (+14.75% over blur-only, PASSES 15% threshold), 1:4 0.03756+-0.00030 (+10.4%, PASSES). Neither mixed arm satisfies BOTH criteria (FID fails for both) -> gate FAILED, no promotion. blur-only itself generates very poorly (FID ~388 vs gaussian ~173) -- blur corruption is far more destructive to unconditional generation than masking was.

needs_user_input.prompt: Recovery convergence-curve diagnostic done (2026-07-19): the cutoff-0.10 delta_G advantage (gaussian vs G+M) is real and persists to 1000 steps (4x the 250-step horizon), never reversing, but PEAKS at 250 (+0.0053) and SHRINKS ~40% by 1000 (+0.0032) -- gaussian partially catches up given more compute. MSE for gaussian/GM overshoots badly past step 250 (drift) even as LPIPS keeps marginally improving. Critical caveat: qualitative grids show NONE of the 3 models achieve recognizable object-identity recovery at cutoff 0.10 at any step -- this is 'which model fails less', not successful recovery. Conclusion: extending recovery steps is NOT a lever to grow the effect (it shrinks it). Full writeup in pi-updates.md 2026-07-19. Separately, the still-open n=8 stopping-rule decision for cutoff 0.4181 (severity sweep, flagged 2026-07-16) remains pending. Next step per user's own brainstorm session is retraining-based levers (ratio sweep / mask-prob tune / scale) if user wants to pursue growing the effect further -- each requires a variant proposal + mechanism check before code, per standing discipline.
